name: Stove-Top Beans
recipeInstructions:
4 servings of about 1 cup each (296 calories per serving)
1. Add beans to boiling water. Boil for 2 minutes. Remove from heat, cover, and soak for 1 hour or overnight in refrigerator.
2. Add salt. Bring beans to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and bring to gentle boil until tender - 1 to 1-1/2 hours.
3. Drain. Save 1 cup bean cooking liquid. Mix with beans and remaining ingredients in saucepan. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and bring to gentle boil for 30 minutes.
4. Continue cooking, uncovered, until sauce reaches desired consistency - about 10 minutes.
5. Serve half of the beans and refrigerate remaining 2 cups for use at another meal within 3 to 4 days.
NOTE: To substitute canned navy beans, use 3-1/4 cups drained; omit boiling water and salt and steps 1 and 2. Use 1 cup water instead of bean cooking liquid. Combine with other ingredients and proceed as directed in step 3.
NOTE: Reheat over medium heat until bubbling, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
